executive_summary: Restaurant food waste is a well-documented, high-pain problem with abundant evidence of real suffering and active spending on solutions.
score_reasoning: Score 75/100 — Complaint Volume: 14/20 (22 unique threads across Reddit, Quora, ChefTalk, G2, industry forums). Complaint Recency: 15/20 (~75% from last 24 months, several from last 6 months). Pain Intensity: 12/20 (17 pain markers, 4 quantified costs, no explicit WTP statement). Workaround Evidence: 16/20 (7 distinct workarounds, 2+ involving multi-tool stacks). Paid Alternatives: 18/20 (14 paid products found, 5+ with 50+ reviews). Total: 14+15+12+16+18=75.

data.problem_statement: Restaurant owners and managers lack predictive tools to align food purchasing and menu planning with actual demand, leading to 4-10% of purchased food being wasted before reaching customers, costing the US restaurant industry $162 billion annually and individual restaurants $333/day on average at a 5% waste rate.
data.target_user: Restaurant owners, managers, and kitchen operators — especially mid-size and multi-location establishments that purchase significant food volumes and lack sophisticated inventory/demand forecasting systems.
data.evidence_summary: 22 unique complaint threads found across Reddit (r/KitchenConfidential, r/restaurantowners), Quora, ChefTalk, G2 reviews, and industry publications. Pattern: restaurant operators consistently cite lack of demand forecasting, manual inventory processes consuming hours, and 4-10% food cost waste as top operational pain points. Multiple quantified costs ($333/day, $50K/year, $1K freezer failure). 14 paid alternatives exist, confirming strong willingness to pay. 7 distinct workarounds documented, from spreadsheets to multi-tool data export chains.
